title: Inscription recording architect of the Bouleuterion

editionAuthor.title: Inscriptiones Segestanae. Le iscrizioni greche e latine di Segesta
editionAuthor.author: Ampolo, Erdas
editionAuthor.citation: Carmine Ampolo e Donatella Erdas, Inscriptiones Segestanae. Le iscrizioni greche e latine di Segesta (Pisa: Edizioni della Normale, 2019)

support: Five slabs from the pavement of the bouleuterion, confirmed to come from the area of the entrance of the portico, of which the first (to the left) is uninscribed, the other four bearing four lines of Greek letters. The individual slabs are 74 cm in width.

handNote.lettering: Large, thinly carved letters of wide module. Alpha with broken bar; epsilon with equal width horizontal strokes; kappa with short arms; theta and omicron full size; theta with detached cross-bar; pi with overlapping cross-bar; sigma with horizontal outer strokes, shallow inner strokes; omicron rhomboid and closed.

date._: end of the second century BCE on archaeological grounds.

provenanceFound._: The second slab from the left first observed by Gualtherus in the ruins of the site; the other four slabs were found in excavation in 1997 of the collapse of a room of mediaeval date constructed on top of the bouletuerion, except for the first, uninscribed slab, which was in re-use in a near-by cistern.

location.address: Case Barbaro - contrada Barbaro S.R. 22, 91013 Calatafimi
